\subtitle{SURV 745 - PracTools Project\\
Team Neyman \textbar{} Detroit Transportation Security Survey}
\author{}
\date{\vspace{-2.5em}April 11, 2026}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\section{Overview}\label{overview}

This file constitutes the \textbf{second-stage (SSU) area sampling
frame} for the Detroit Transportation Security Survey. It lists all
census block groups associated with the eligible census tracts in the
Stage 1 frame (FRAME1\_NEYMAN.csv). Block groups serve as secondary
sampling units (SSUs): within each selected tract, exactly one block
group is selected using systematic PPS sampling with the
block-group-level composite MOS.

We obtained the block-group data from the 2020 Decennial Census DHC
Summary File (table P12) for Wayne County, Michigan. Only block groups
nested within tracts that passed the Stage 1 eligibility threshold of at
least 50 adults aged 18+ are included in this frame.

\section{Variable Descriptions}\label{variable-descriptions}

Block Group FIPS & 12-digit census block group FIPS code \\
Tract FIPS & 11-digit parent tract FIPS code (links to
FRAME1\_NEYMAN.csv) \\
Tot. HH & Total occupied housing units in the block group (2020
Census) \\
Tot. Persons & Total resident population in the block group (2020
Census) \\
Adults 18+ & Total civilian non-institutionalized persons aged 18 and
older \\
18-34 & Population aged 18--34 \\
35-49 & Population aged 35--49 \\
50-64 & Population aged 50--64 \\
65+ & Population aged 65 and older \\
Comp. MOS & Composite measure of size: \(\sum_g f_g N_{gj}\), where
\(f_g\) is the domain-specific sampling fraction and \(N_{gj}\) is the
domain population in block group \(j\) \\
\end{longtable}

\section{Notes}\label{notes}

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{Source:} 2020 Decennial Census DHC Summary File, table P12
  (Sex by Age), accessed via the \texttt{tidycensus} R package for Wayne
  County, MI. Block groups were matched to Detroit tract FIPS codes and
  filtered to retain only those nested within eligible tracts.
\item
  \textbf{Linkage to Stage 1 frame:} Every block group in this file
  belongs to a tract listed in FRAME1\_NEYMAN.csv. The
  \texttt{Tract\ FIPS} column is the key for joining the two frame
  files.
\item
  \textbf{Composite MOS:} The block-group-level composite MOS is used
  for conditional PPS selection at Stage 2 (one block group per selected
  tract). Within a given tract, the conditional selection probability
  for block group \(j\) is
  \(\pi_{2j|i} = MOS_{ij} / \sum_{\ell \in i} MOS_{i\ell}\). For tracts
  containing only one block group, that block group is selected with
  certainty (\(\pi_{2j|i} = 1\)).
\item
  \textbf{Block groups with zero MOS:} If a block group has zero adults
  in all four age domains (e.g., a purely commercial or institutional
  area), its composite MOS is zero. In the sample selection code, such
  block groups within a multi-BG tract receive equal selection
  probability (\(1/n_{BGs}\)) as a fallback to ensure the tract can
  still contribute an SSU.
\item
  \textbf{Population counts:} Age group counts are derived from P12
  series variables in the DHC file and reflect the 2020 Census
  enumeration. They serve as proxy measures of the current eligible
  population for frame construction and MOS assignment only.
\end{enumerate}

\end{document}
